title: 4.7 Red-Black Trees
subtitle: Color rules, balancing logic, and extension-ready visualization/code/practice

content.introduction: Red-Black trees use color rules and controlled rotations to maintain near-balance with efficient updates.

sections.title: 4.7.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define core properties: root black, no consecutive reds, equal black-height on all root-to-leaf paths.
examples: Check whether a colored tree satisfies Red-Black rules

sections.title: 4.7.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe insertion/deletion fixes using recoloring and rotations across parent-uncle configurations.
examples: Fix insertion with red uncle by recoloring
Fix insertion with black uncle by rotation

sections.title: 4.7.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Operations are O(log n) with fewer strict constraints than AVL, often yielding lower rebalancing overhead in updates.
examples: Compare AVL and Red-Black update patterns
Prefer Red-Black for balanced mixed workloads

keyTakeaways: Use Red-Black trees in standard library map/set contexts
